role_hints.oews_basis: 41-3011 Advertising Sales Agents 8.2% of wage bill in the 4-digit 5418 proxy — for this code (firms selling ad space/time on behalf of media owners) sales agents are the dominant real occupation, est. ~35-45% of the actual wage bill including tail sales staff and 41-3091 Sales Representatives of Services 3.0%
role_hints.note: Relationship selling to agencies and advertisers on behalf of station/publisher clients is the product itself; AI assists prospecting, proposals and avails packaging but the negotiated sale persists — where it persists at all (see terminal question).

role_hints.oews_basis: 13-1161 Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists 6.6% (proxy) + research/planner tail est. ~4-6%
role_hints.note: Ratings/audience research decks, avails, one-sheets and proposal packaging are directly automatable.

role_hints.oews_basis: 11-1021 General and Operations Managers 9.3% + 11-2021 Marketing Managers 5.2% (proxy)
role_hints.note: Relationships with represented media owners (the rep contracts) and key agency accounts persist; internal management automates.

role_hints.role: Order management, traffic and back office
role_hints.oews_basis: not in listed top-10; order entry, traffic, billing and admin staff in the tail, est. ~15-20%
role_hints.note: Insertion orders, make-goods, invoicing and reconciliation — directly automatable, and already being displaced by programmatic pipes.

role_hints.oews_basis: 4-digit top-10 covers ~51% of wage bill and includes occupations from sibling codes (27-3031 PR Specialists 8.3%, 27-1024 Graphic Designers 3.3%, 27-1011 Art Directors 4.1%, 41-9011 Demonstrators 1.6%, 27-1026 Merchandise Displayers 1.2%) that are not media-rep jobs — treat as contamination
role_hints.note: Misc. finance/IT/admin; allocate explicitly rather than inheriting proxy shares.

source_hints.source: BIA Advisory Services local advertising forecasts
source_hints.why: Sizes local broadcast/print ad markets — the demand base rep firms sell into, and its secular decline.

source_hints.source: RAB (Radio Advertising Bureau) and TVB (Television Bureau of Advertising) revenue data
source_hints.why: National/local spot revenue trends — the commission base for broadcast rep firms; check whether either publishes rep-firm-specific data.

source_hints.source: IAB / eMarketer programmatic and ad-automation research
source_hints.why: Quantifies the shift from human-repped sales to programmatic pipes — the substitution mechanism for this code.

source_hints.source: Trade press on rep-firm consolidation (Katz Media/iHeart lineage, publisher-rep roll-ups)
source_hints.why: The national rep business consolidated to near-monopoly decades ago — history relevant to whether any acquirable independent tier remains; verify current ownership structures.

source_hints.source: Census Business Trends and Outlook Survey (BTOS) AI supplement
source_hints.why: Generic AI-adoption anchor in the absence of any rep-industry survey.

capture_questions: Rep firms earn commission on ad sales made for represented media owners (a % of billings placed) — hour-savings from AI flow to the operator by construction, but the commission base is the question: are media owners cutting rep commissions, taking sales in-house, or shifting inventory to programmatic channels that pay reps nothing? Who keeps AI savings is secondary to whether the commission base survives.

Who owns the relationship — the rep firm's contracts with media owners (typically exclusive but terminable) or individual salespeople carrying agency relationships? What do rep-contract terms and turnover show?

What stops media owners from replacing reps with programmatic/self-serve sales stacks (SSPs, direct deals platforms) plus one in-house seller — the media owner is the client here, and their bypass calculus is the moat question.
Is there a defensible niche (local broadcast political spend, specialty publishers, out-of-home) where human repped sales holds pricing power against automated channels?
terminal_value_question: The mechanism deciding terminal value is programmatic disintermediation of a declining medium: rep firms sell traditional media inventory (broadcast spot, print) whose ad dollars are shrinking secularly, while automated pipes replace the human rep function on what remains. Mature AI accelerates both. Is there any terminal paid demand for third-party human media representation — e.g., political broadcast spend, complex sponsorships — or does the operator's product disappear regardless of how cheaply AI lets them run? Do not let AI-driven cost savings mask a melting-revenue-base problem; separate the two explicitly.
special_notes: Structural-decline trap (primary): this code's demand base (traditional media ad spend sold via reps) has been shrinking for two decades independent of AI — score V on the automatable wage bill honestly, but scoring B/M on historical revenue or multiples without a decline adjustment is the single most likely error here.

Small-N red flag: n_total is 1,163, n_band is only 50, and the 100-499/500+ classes fall OUT of the band per the record's chain — the acquirable universe is extremely thin; a roll-up thesis may fail on N alone before any scoring subtlety matters.

Capture is pricing-model-favorable in form (commission on sales, not hours) but the deflation channel is commission-rate pressure and in-housing by represented media owners — model pi_dist through the media-owner's bypass decision, not client-fee rescoping.

Revenue accounting: distinguish commission income from gross billings placed; the record's revenue/employee ($300k) suggests receipts are mostly commission (net) but verify before using revenue-based comparisons.

Consolidation history distorts comps: national TV/radio rep is effectively consolidated (Katz Media, owned by iHeartMedia, dominant for decades) — remaining independents are niche publisher/digital reps; do not use national-rep economics as comps for band-sized firms.

Role-mix contamination: the 4-digit 5418 proxy imports PR, creative and display occupations that do not exist in rep firms — rebuild roles around sales, sales support and order operations as above and say so.
Boundary confusion: media buying (demand side) is 541830, ad agencies are 541810, and digital ad networks/SSPs sit in 519/5182 codes — surveys of 'media sales' rarely isolate this code.
